outline.title: WITHDRAWAL OF PREVIOUS MOTION AND REMARKS OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (P)
CROSS-EXAMINATION BY THE COUNSEL FOR THE RESPONDENT

blocks.text: Republic of the Philippines
SENATE
Pasay City
JOURNAL
Senate Sitting as An IMPEACHMENT COURT On the Impeachment Trial oe Vice-President Sara Zimmerman Duterte
IMPEACHMENT TRIAL NO. 4
Wednesday, 8 July 2026
PREPARED BY THE Legislative Journal Service Senate of the Philippines
IMPEACHMENT TRIAL NO. 4
Wedneesday, July 8, 2026
CALL TO ORDER

At 2:00 p.m., Wednesday, July 8, 2026, the Senate President, Hon. Win Gatchalian, with the Senate sitting as an Impeachment Court, called the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte to order.
PRAYER

blocks.speaker: Sen. Pia S
blocks.text: Cayetano led the prayer, to wit:
Heavenly Father, we come before You today with a deep sense of responsibility.

We ask for Your wisdom. Lord; the kind of wisdom that seeks only what is true and what is right. As Senator-Judges, may we listen carefully, reason clearly, and decide justly. May we never lose sight of the fact that our role here is not to serve any interest but the interest of the Filipino people and the integrity of our institutions. We pray for fairness in every step of this process. May the truth be heard, may the evidence speak for itself, and may justice, whatever form it takes, be arrived at with honesty and clear conscience.

We also pray for our nation. We know that moments like this can divide us. May this process, difficult as it is, bring us closer to the kind of Philippines we all want to live in- one where no one is above the law and where every Filipino can trust that justice is real.
In Your word. Book of Timothy 2:1-2, Timothy urges the people to pray, petition, and intercede for their leaders. We are a prayerful nation, O Lord, please hear our prayers.
In Jesus’ Name. Amen.
ROLL CALL

Upon direction of the Senate President, the Clerk of Court, Atty. Renato N. Bantug Jr., called the roll, to which the following senator-judges responded: Aquino, B. Cayetano, A. P. S. Cayetano, P. S. Ejercito, J. V. Escudero, F. G. Gatchalian, W. T. Go, C. L. T. Hontiveros, R. Lacson, P. M. Lapid, M. M.
Legarda, L. Padilla, R. Pangilinan, F. N. Sotto III, V. C. Tulfo, E. T. Tulfo, R. T. Villanueva, J. Villar, C. A. Villar, M. A. Zubiri, J. M. F.
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8, 2026
With 20 senators present, the Chair declared the presence of a quorum.
Senator-Judge Marcos was also present.
Senator-Judges Estrada and Marcoleta were under detention and unable to attend.
Senator-Judge Dela Rosa was absent.
PROCLAMATION OF THE SERGEANT-AT-ARMS

The Sergeant-at-Arms, PMGen Alfred S. Corpus, commanded all persons, on pain of penalty, to keep silent while the Senate is sitting for the trial of the Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Duterte.
APPROVAL OF THE JOURNAL

Upon motion of the Presiding Officer, Sen. Francis “Chiz” G. Escudero, there being no objection, the Body dispensed with the reading of the Journal of the Senate sitting as an Impeachment Court on July 7, 2026 and considered the same as approved.
CALLING OF THE CASE

Upon direction of the Presiding Officer, the Clerk of Court, Atty. Renato N. Bantug Jr., called Case No. 004-2026, entitled “In the Matter of the Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte. ”
APPEARANCES
The Presiding Officer directed the parties to enter their appearances.
For the prosecution. Prosecutor Gerville Luistro entered the appearances previously entered, on behalf of the prosecution.
For the Respondent, Atty. Sheila Sison entered the same appearances.
PRESENTATION OF WITNESS
Presiding Officer Escudero directed the Clerk of Court to call in Agent John Mark Calilung of the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) to continue his testimony from the previous day’s proceedings.
It was noted for the record that Atty. Amando Virgil D. Ligutan appeared as counsel for the prosecution, while Atty. Carlo Joaquin Narvasa appeared as counsel for the respondent.
ORDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

blocks.speaker: Presiding Officer Escudero recalled that, during the previous day’s proceedings
blocks.text: Attorney Ligutan delivered a statement in response to a question from a Senator-Judge, prompting Senator- Judge Cayetano (P) to move that the remarks be stricken from the record.

The Presiding Officer observed, however, that since the proceedings were televised, striking the remarks from the transcript would serve no practical purpose. Accordingly, in the interest of fairness, he directed that respondent’s counsel, Atty. Narvasa, be given an equivalent
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8, 2026
opportunity of four minutes and 26 seconds to make a statement after the completion of the cross- examination.

The Chair further directed that similar perorations by counsel would no longer be permitted in future proceedings and reminded the Senator-Judges to allow witnesses, rather than counsel, to present the evidence.
REMARKS OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) commended the Presiding Officer for his prudence in handling the matter and emphasized that Senator-Judges should refrain from asking questions that could prejudice the proceedings. He agreed that striking the prosecution’s earlier remarks from the record would serve little purpose since the proceedings had already been televised.

He maintained that the Respondent’s counsel should be afforded the same opportunity previously extended to the prosecution, with reasonable latitude to address both the question raised and matters relating to its affirmative defenses.
WITHDRAWAL OF PREVIOUS MOTION AND REMARKS OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (P)

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) withdrew her previous motion, agreeing with the Presiding Officer’s ruling
blocks.text: She underscored the importance of strict adherence to the Rules, stating that orderly proceedings depend on their consistent application. She explained that raising procedural objections and calling the attention of the Presiding Officer whenever the Rules appear to have been violated are necessary to uphold due process and ensure the proper administration of justice.
REMARKS OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER
Presiding Officer Escudero acknowledged the remarks of Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) and issued procedural reminders to ensure the orderly conduct of the proceedings.

He announced that a 15-minute recess would be called midway through the hearing to allow the parties to relax, go over their notes and also to allow Senator-Judges and counsel who were not actively participating to briefly leave the Session Hall in a non-disruptive manner. He likewise reminded the Senator-Judges to keep their conversations at a low volume to avoid distracting others.
REMINDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER
Before the proceedings resumed. Presiding Officer Escudero reminded Agent Calilung that his testimony and cross-examination would continue under the oath previously administered.
CROSS-EXAMINATION BY THE COUNSEL FOR THE RESPONDENT
Thereupon, the Presiding Officer allowed Attorney Narvasa (C) to cross-examine the witness, to wit:

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Thank you.
Counsel for respondent, Atty. Narvasa, if you’re ready, you may proceed with your cross- examination.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Your Honor, may I be allowed to approach the witness?
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8,2026

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: You may do so, Counsel.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you, Your Honor.
Good afternoon, Mr. Calilung.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Good afternoon. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: During your direct examination, you testified that you were trained in photo and video authentication.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: You said that you were trained for two weeks.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Specific for photo and video, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Sir, my questions are answerable by a ‘yes’ or a ‘no.’

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Can you please constrain yourself to these questions unless I tell you, all right?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Objection to that, Your Honor, please.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: And you got this training from....

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, objection to that.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Before Counsel for respondent proceeds. Counsel for the prosecution, let the Presiding Officer to recognize you first.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Thank you.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: You are so recognized. Sir. What is your objection?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Thank you so much.

In as much as I would like to accommodate the defense that in asking the question that the witness is to be limited to a ‘yes’ or ‘no’ question. Your Honor, please. Unfortunately, Your Honor, please, that is not sanctioned in the Rules. There is nothing in our rules that would allow a defense to constrain a witness, even on cross-examination, to answer the questions with a ‘yes’ or ‘no’ answer. It is not in the rules.

So, Your Honor, please, we caimot constrain a witness to answering a ‘yes’ or a ‘no.’ The witness should be able to qualify. The usual answer by the defense is that it could be handled by the prosecution on redirect, but that is not the case. Your Honor, please. So long as there is nothing in the Rules that would allow the defense to constrain the witness to answer ‘yes’ or ‘no,’ the prosecution will vehemently object to that. Your Honor, please.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: May I, Your Honor?

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: The Presiding Officer overrules the objection.

The counsel for prosecutors can make a continuing objection regarding this matter. But it is the position of the Presiding Officer that the witness is seasoned enough. According to his testimony yesterday, he has attended over—if I recall correctly—30 hearings already. So, he would know how to respond and to explain if the need would arise. And it is up for counsel for the respondent, if at all, to strike out whatever answers that are not responsive to his question.
Counsel for respondent, may proceed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: We would abide. Your Honor. We would abide.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Thank you. Counsel for the prosecutors.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: And you testified that you got this training from MaxCor Inter-Ventures, correct?
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: And you said that the training you got from MaxCor Inter-Ventures was in digital forensics?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: I have with me an Article of Partnership of MaxCor Inter-Ventures, Co.. Do you see that it states that it is the Articles of Partnership of MaxCor Inter-Ventures Co.?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, may we allow Atty. Stephen Magallon to also approach the witness stand to peruse the document?
And, of course, at this juncture. Your Honor, please, I have to object.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: I have no question yet. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: I am objecting to the witness being shown an Articles of Partnership, your Honor, please.
Anyway, since the question has not been asked, I will....

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Let us wait for the question. Counsel.
In the meantime, the Presiding Officer allows Counsel for the prosecutors to examine the document being presented to the witness.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: And, Your Honor, for the reference of the senators, we are flashing this Articles of Partnership on the screens.

Sir, please take a look at page 2 of these Articles. Let us read it together, all right? Primary purpose: to engage in the business of trading both import and export of goods on wholesale retail basis. Do you see that. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Now, on the very same page 2, the secondary purpose. Let us read it together again: “own, use, improve, develop, subdivide, sell, exchange, lease of all kinds including buildings, houses, apartments, and other structures.” Do you see that. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: After that, in the same page 2, the second secondary purpose is to “print, publish, distribute, buy, sell, acquire rights, to and generally deal in all kinds of software packages, books, periodicals, newspapers, magazines, journals, and other publications dealing in or having to do with subjects of all kinds, character, and all acts of things as may time to time be deemed needful, necessary, or required.” Do you see that. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you. Sir.
Now I am showing you a screenshot of the website of this MaxCor Inter-Venmres, Co. It states under “About us”—I am also flashing this on the screen. Your Honor, for the ease of the senator- judges.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, at this juncture, the prosecution will now manifest that there was no question propounded as to the presentation of the Articles of Partnership. And the counsel is now moving forward to the presentation of an alleged website.
Therefore, it seems that the defense counsel is the one presenting the documents without propounding any question to this witness; highly improper. Your Honor, please; highly improper.
The Counsel should be asking question and not presenting document. You will have your turn when the defense team is allowed to present your evidence.
Two documents, no question yet. Highly improper. Your Honor, please. Again, I do not want to put the lawyer on the spot, but that is highly improper.
Ask questions.
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8,2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Your Honor, I did ask questions.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: No, there was no question.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Can we ask that the transcript be read back?

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel, kindly proceed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for the prosecutors, with the understanding that Mr. Calilung is not testifying as to the truth of whatever counsel asked him or what he read because, clearly, he is not competent to testify as to the truth of what is contained in the articles of partnership. However, as to the fact that he read it and saw it, up to that extent, and the Sena tor-Judges are so advised as well. So, ordered.
You may proceed. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you. Sir. And I am on cross here. Your Honor. Thank you.

Sir, under the words “About Us,” it says “MaxCor Inter-Ventures has been established as a one-stop source of different products and services intended for both government and private institutions. Its company tagline, “Innovation without Boundaries,” stands true testament to provide customers with quality merchandise: from the simplest to the most sophisticated ones at very affordable prices. The Company places a long-term business relationship through customer satisfaction on top of its priority list. With integrity and honesty as the foundation of the company, it envisions to evolve into excellence amidst the competitive pace of information technology.” Do you see that. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Sir, now I am showing you another screenshot of the website of this MaxCor Inter-Ventures, Co. You confirm that—I am also flashing this for the ease of the Court. Sir—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: At this point. Your Honor, please allow—the prosecution is allowed—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for the prosecutor is recognized.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, not by way of an objection but a manifestation. Your Honor, please.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Your Honor, this is—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait. I am not done.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Before that, I would like to object to your manifestation.
Your Honor, there is no objection. Why is the prosecutor allowed to manifest during my cross- examination?

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for respondent, kindly wait for the Presiding Officer to recognize you.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: What is the desire of the counsel for the prosecutors? To make a—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: 1 would like to put into record the simple fact. Your Honor, please, that basis has not yet been laid as to the presentation of these documents. He presented an article of partnership and screenshots of websites without saying who took these screenshots or from where the defense got the articles of partnership. That is only by way of manifestation. No need for the court to rule on that, Your Honor, please.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Noted. Earlier ruling of the Presiding Officer stands. Counsel may proceed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Your Honor, may 1 respond?

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: No need. Counsel. It was not an objection. Please proceed with your cross.
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you, Your Honor. May I also state a manifestation, Your Honor? Your Honor, the basis of the defense was laid—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: For the record, the Chair will allow it just this one, please.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: The basis of the defense was laid when we asked him whether he testified that he got his training as an expert witness from MaxCor Inter-Ventures. That is basic, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel, please proceed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you. Your Honor.

Sir, could you take a look at this? Under the words “Our Products,” the products seen there are “Office Supplies and Equipment,” “ICT Equipment (Software and Hardware),” “Investigative Equipment (Hardware and Software),” “Emergency Response Software and CCTVs.” Do you see that?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Thank you. Mr. Calilung, are you a lawyer?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: No, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Yesterday, you testified that you signed the affidavit of investigation dated February 10, 2025.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Your Honor, may I request a copy from the prosecution? I think it is Exhibit P-IV-3 in series.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: “3” series.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, please. Manifestation: The witness, number one, mentioned the affidavit of investigation, but the witness did not identify the documents. So, for the record. Your Honor, please, that document is not yet presented in Court as a documentary evidence. Your Honor, please. So, highly improper for the defense to elicit that document through this witness, precisely because this witness did not yet identify the affidavit of investigation. Your Honor, please.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Which exhibit is this? I believe he did. Counsel, but which exhibit is this?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Exhibit P-IV-3 to Exhibit P-IV-3bb, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, please, if I may. It was not yet identified, but it is pre-marked. Your Honor, please, to be presented by our next witness, Atty. Jeremy Lotoc. So, highly improper, inasmuch as we want to accommodate the defense—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: So, who is the signatory in that document? Is it—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Your Honor, Mr. Calilung is a signatory to that document.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Is one of the signatories.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Yes.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Is one of the signatories. But the counsel for prosecutors is correct. This document has not yet been identified by any of the witnesses for the counsel for the prosecutors. You will get your chance, counsel for the respondent, when Attorney Lotoc testifies later, which I believe this is the only exhibit that he will be testifying to.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: All right. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Kindly proceed. Counsel.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: You mentioned a—affidavit of investigation, correct?
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8.2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: And this affidavit of investigation, you sent a copy to the House of Representatives’ Committee on Justice?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: You identified it during the House Committee hearings on April 29, 2025?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: I am showing you now an affidavit of investigation. This is marked as Exhibit D-IV-46 and series, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: “D-IV-46” and series. Is there a counterpart marking from the counsel for prosecutors?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor. This is “P-IV-3” to P-IV-3bb.”

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Thank you. For the record.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: This affidavit of investigation has appendages, correct— Annexes?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: And it forms a whole, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Okay. Sir, do you confirm that you signed this?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: I am one of the signatories. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Your Honor, do you have—here, Sir. Kaso long, Sir, iyong sinabmit (submit) mo po—I withdraw. Your Honor.

Apart from this affidavit of investigation dated February 10, are you aware if there is another affidavit of investigation executed by the NBI represented by the Cybercrime Division in relation to this case?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: This is the only one that is submitted. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: No. Executed. Signed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: My question is executed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: —the defense cannot argue with the witness. Your Honor, please.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: The witness would know how to respond. Counsel, if he is not responsive to your question, do not argue with the witness. Can you rephrase?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.
Apart from this affidavit of investigation, are you aware if there is any other affidavit of investigation signed by the NBI represented by the Cybercrime Division?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. At that juncture, I have to object. Vague, Your Honor. Affidavit of investigation signed by the NBI. there could be thousands of documents.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Related to this case. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: If the witness knows, witness may answer. Is there any other affidavit of investigation executed by the NBI Cybercrime in relation to this case, witness?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: As far as I remember. Sir, this is the final affidavit of investigation. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Final affidavit. Are you sure, agent? Let me remind you that you are under oath.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes. Sir.
WEDNESDAY. JULY 8,2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: I have with me a certification—a DOJ certification executed by Jayvee Lawrence B. Bandong—Senior Assistant State Prosecutor Jayvee Bandong.
For the record, Your Honor, this is Exhibit D-IV-29 for the defense.
Do you see this, Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: This certification, dated February 5, 2025, states, and I quote: “The referral— The complaint/referral does not contain all the necessary evidence to prove the essential elements of the crime. Therefore, it is recommended that the same be first referred for case build-up." Do you see that?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Now, below that, in the second page, under the word “NOTES:” number 1, therefore, states and I quote that, “There is no—” may I have this flashed on the screen, please? For the ease of the Court so the Honorable Court can follow me.

Under “NOTES:” again. Sir—for the record. Your Honor, we are flashing the certification on the screen. Under “NOTES:” Sir, number 1, therefore, states and I quote: “There is no affidavit of the person/s who recorded the video entitled "VP November 23, PressCon at Batasan” through Open Broadcast Software (OBS) and in turn, stored it in a digital video disc (DVD).” Do you see that. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Let’s go to page 2. On paragraph 7, it states, and I quote, “One of the elements of the crime of Grave Threats is that the offender threatens another with the infliction upon the latter’s person, honor, or property or upon that of the latter’s family of any wrong amounting to any crime. In the instant complaint, only the NBI executed an affidavit and there was no participation on the part of the offended parties. As it appears, NBI is not the offended party for the crime of Grave Threats.” Do you see that?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: Above this, in paragraph 6, it states that, and I quote: “Only the minutes of the meeting with several personalities and representative from various media outfits were submitted. Although the minutes were signed, there was no individual affidavit executed regarding the contents of said minutes.” Do you see that. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: On paragraph 5, it states that, and I quote: “The affidavit of one Patria Morales mentioned that she stored these footages in a USB and submitted it to the NBI. However, there was no USB attached to the complaint, (only a DVD was attached - there was no detail regarding the contents of this DVD).” Do you see that. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: On paragraph 4, it states, and I quote: “Aimex L did not indicate who made such TSN. There was also no affidavit of the person who made the TSN.” Do you see that?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: On paragraph 3, it states that, and I quote: “Annexes I and K (DVDs with markings NBI-CNN-C-24-12436-2 and NBl-CCN-C-24-12436-2) were not accompanied by an affidavit of the record and TSN (also affidavit of the one who will execute the TSN).”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Narvasa (C)
blocks.text: On paragraph 2. it states, and I quote: “The press briefing conducted on October 18 was not recorded and/or stored in a DVD (no video submitted) and there was no TSN of the said briefing and the necessary affidavit of the person taking the TSN.”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung

REQUESTS OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) seconded the earlier request of Senator-Judge Marcos and further requested that copies of the previous day’s Ruling of the Presiding Officer likewise be furnished to the senator-judges, emphasizing the importance of enabling all Members to closely follow the proceedings. He reiterated the observation of Senator Cayetano (P) that those seated in the front row had difficulty viewing the television monitors and suggested that additional screens be installed in front of the senator-judges.
In response, the Presiding Officer stated that television monitors would be installed in front of the counsels’ tables.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) also proposed utilizing iPads or tablets, with the Secretariat, the Office of the Clerk of Court, and the senators' respective staff coordinating to facilitate access to the documents. To illustrate the need for a more efficient system, he pointed out that page 19 of the Impeachment Complaint, specifically Footnote No. 72, referred to an affidavit allegedly contained in Annex G. He noted, however, that Annex G actually contained a certification and that the affidavit was instead located in Volume 14, Annex FFFFFFFFF to GGGGGGGGG. He further observed that the annexes had been assigned different labels when initially distributed and were later given new exhibit markings after being pre-marked, making the documents difficult to identify.
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8,2026
The Presiding Officer acknowledged the observation.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) clarified that he was not requesting the advance disclosure of documents that might prejudice either party’s presentation or rebuttal. He proposed that copies, whether in digital or printed form, be furnished to the senator-judges as they are shown to the witnesses, to facilitate a clearer understanding of the proceedings. He clarified that he was not proposing that the parties provide 24 printed copies of every document. Noting that the hearing was being held on a Wednesday, he suggested that, beginning the following day, the Office of the Clerk of Court coordinate with the respective staff of the Senator-Judges to develop an appropriate system for providing access to the documents.

In response, the Presiding Officer stated that the senator-judges would be provided, on a per- article basis, with electronic copies of the prosecution’s pre-marked evidence for Article IV through their official email addresses for viewing on their iPads or laptops. He added, however, that the documents presented by Counsel for the Respondent had not yet been pre-marked, although copies could nevertheless be requested from the Respondent’s counsel for the guidance of the senator- judges, without prejudice to the Court’s subsequent determination of whether such documents would be formally offered in evidence.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) replied that even if the documents had already been pre-marked, he would have no objection to receiving them only in real time if earlier disclosure might prejudice a party’s presentation. He likewise requested that documents used during cross-examination, recross-examination, or rebuttal—even if not pre-marked—be made available to the senator-judges in real time so that they could review them during the proceedings rather than only after adjournment, which could affect their preparation for the next hearing.

The Presiding Officer then asked respondent’s counsel whether such arrangement would likewise be feasible once the parties’ roles were reversed. In response. Attorney Narvasa clarified that all the documents shown during the day’s proceedings had already been pre-marked by the respondent.

The Presiding Officer remarked that the response differed from his understanding, noting that the DOJ letter presented appeared to be different from the DOJ letter contained in the Court’s pre-marked records. Relative thereto, he stated that the matter would be verified.

Attorney Narvasa, however, replied that all the documents had been pre-marked. The Presiding Officer then observed that one document appeared to have been presented without any marking by either party, to which Attorney Narvasa again maintained that all the documents he presented had been pre-marked.

The Presiding Officer stated that if all the documents had indeed been pre-marked, there would be no objection to furnishing copies thereof to the senator-judges. Attorney Narvasa confirmed that the Respondent’s counsel had no objection to such an arrangement. The Presiding Officer further remarked that Senator-Judge Cayetano (A)’s concern would effectively be addressed if all the documents had in fact been pre-marked, adding that ensuring the proper identification and distribution of the documents was the responsibility of the Court. He then inquired whether the Prosecution could likewise fiimish copies of its documentary evidence, to which Attorney Ligutan replied in the affirmative.

Presiding Officer Escudero additionally observed that Counsel for the Respondent had been helpful in displaying the affidavits on the courtroom monitors during the presentation and encouraged the Prosecution to adopt the same practice.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) sought clarification as to whether the prescribed two-minute limit applied only to asking questions and excluded the witness’s responses. The Presiding Officer confirmed that the two-minute allotment pertained solely to the asking of questions.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) further requested that the allotted time likewise exclude instances where the witness would ask to repeat the question. While acknowledging that two minutes might be sufficient for brief inquiries, he observed that many senator-judges wished to examine the issues more thoroughly rather than ask only one or two questions. In response, the Presiding Officer assured Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) that the Court would apply the two-minute rule with reasonable leniency.
MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) emphasized the importance of ensuring that the questioning remained appropriate in light of both the previous days’ proceedings and the current hearing. He stated that he would not object if either counsel respectfully called attention to a question that appeared to be improperly framed or that might prejudice the case in favor of either party.

He clarified, however, that while counsel could not correct a senator-judge, he welcomed respectful observations whenever counsel believed that a question might prejudice the proceedings, recognizing that doing so formed part of counsel’s duty to protect the interests of the parties represented.

blocks.text: Ginoong Tagapamahala ng Hukumang ito, nais ko lamang po sana hingin ang mga credential ng ating saksi, kung papaano po lahat ng kaniyang certification, upang mapatunayan po natin lahat.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: The witness testified to it earlier but kindly respond to the question of Senator-Judge Padilla. Mr. witness.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: I have here. Sir, my certifications during the time of my investigation. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Mahaba ba?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: I can read. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Mahaba ba? Hindi?

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Padilla
blocks.text: Ibigay na long po siguro sa atin.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Kindly just submit it to us. Yes, please.

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge PadUla
blocks.text: Opo, submit na lang po.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.
Thank you. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: If that is okay with Senator-Judge Padilla.

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Padilla
blocks.text: Opo.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Thank you. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung, you are excused
blocks.text: Sir. Thank you for your time and cooperation with the Impeachment Court.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Calilung
blocks.text: Thank you. Your Honor.
Thereafter, the Presiding Officer discharged the witness.
STATEMENT OF THE RESPONDENT’S COUNSEL

Pursuant to the agreement of the parties and the earlier order of the Presiding Officer, respondent’s counsel, Atty. Vinluan, was afforded the same period of four minutes and 26 seconds previously granted to the prosecution to address the issue of whether the acts alleged in Article IV constitute an impeachable offense, as follows: “In the discourse yesterday, talking about VP Sara’s statements during the November 23, 2024 press con. Senator-Judge Risa Hontiveros stated, and I quote:
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8,2026
“None of these statements are proof that the Vice President actually contracted an assassin or hired one.”
She is absolutely right.

She then asked the question: “Why are these acts impeachable?” To this question, the prosecution responded: “We understand that these statements may not actually 100% prove that she, in fact, contracted an assassin.”

Hence, the prosecution admitted that the video does not prove any fact other than its existence. In simple words, there is no proof of any contracting of an assassin. The prosecution’s admission before this Honorable Court made their respondents’ position clear. The prosecution has no other competent evidence to prove that VP Sara contracted an assassin.

Ang testimonya ni Calilung, gaya ng iba pang ebidensya kuno ng prosecution ay hindi sapat para patunayan ang paratang na ito. Trying to prove the stretched narrative using a hodgepodge of materials is a waste of this Honorable Court’s time and the Filipino people’s money. Si VP Sara na mismo ang nagsabi na hindi assassin ang kinausap niya. This term only came from people who took her statements out of their proper context and tried to exaggerate them for what they really meant.

The truth of the matter is this: she and her family were threatened. And while her response was unconventional, it was justified. What triggered her response was a question about Operation Romanov, which is the plot to eliminate VP Sara and her entire family.

The NBI’s own records prove this. In the minutes of the meeting of Dempsey Reyes’ interview, he said, and I quote: "'May press con kasi earlier than that. Sabi lang niya, gusto siyang patayin daw—according to her—ni Speaker. That was during a press conference the day before November 2022.”

During the virtual press con. Princess Maui mentioned Operation Romanov again and even warned her about her safety. Reyes talked about it. Princess Maui reiterated it, but the NBI gaslighted it. When VP Sara uttered those words, she was not responding as Sara Duterte, the Vice President, but Sara Duterte—the wife, mother, daughter, and sister who only sought to protect her own and her family members’ lives. Operation Romanov is in place and the threat against their lives is real.

VP Sara became the target of unauthorized intelligence and surveillance operations by government operatives that exposed her family to high-level risk and security threats thereby causing intense trauma and uncontrollable fear. Her homes in Davao and Manila were profiled and this is proven by classified casing reports. Her trusted security personnel were removed. Her schedule of official functions like the one in Naga was leaked by intelligence agents and her security was heavily compromised.

In sum, the prosecution was right in using the term “culmination.” But the prosecution’s interpretation is wrong. Rather, when VP Sara left the Administration, she suffered various forms of threats and harassment for many months from the powers that be and the culmination was her statement on that fateful early morning in November 2024. She didn’t commit an impeachable offense. She drew the line in the sand and said, “Enough is enough.” That is the meaning and that is the context.

Finally, as to the alleged grave threats and inciting to sedition, these are defined by the Revised Penal Code and are punishable by prision correccional. Thus, these fall under the jurisdiction of the MTC or first level courts. These charges are not other high crimes as contemplated by the Constitution as grounds for impeachment and are therefore not impeachable.

After all, VP Sara was fighting two important battles that day: one, against the threat to her family’s life; and another, against the life of the ordinary Filipino, constantly suffocated by the immense weight of corruption, which she talked about during the press con. She never incited sedition. She merely echoed the voice of ordinary Filipinos.
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8,2026

Her message that day only reflected what was already in their hearts and minds. There was no betrayal of public trust. She actually upheld the people’s trust as she spoke for and behalf of the people.
Thank you, Your Honor.”
MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE AQUINO

Senator-Judge Aquino appealed to both parties to conduct the examination of succeeding witnesses in a more direct and efficient manner, observing that the testimony of the first witness had taken approximately nine hours to complete. While recognizing that each party was entitled to pursue its own litigation strategy, he urged counsel for both parties to allow the witnesses’ testimonies and documentary evidence to be presented more fully so that the issues could be better understood by the Senator-Judges and the public.

He emphasized that his remarks were intended as a request rather than an order and expressed hope that the proceedings would remain focused on the presentation of facts and evidence to facilitate a fair and informed resolution of the case.
MANIFESTATION OF THE PROSECUTION
The prosecution informed the Court that Agent Calilung would be its final witness for the day and that its next witness, Atty. Jeremy Lotoc, would be presented at the next hearing.

Upon clarification by the prosecution panel, the schedule of witnesses for the following week was identified as follows: Atty. Lotoc on Monday, Atty. Zuleika Lopez on Tuesday, and Capt. Belinda Bello on Wednesday, while the presentation of NBI Director Melvin Matibag would depend on the progress of the proceedings.
Accordingly, Presiding Officer Escudero ordered the issuance of subpoenas to Attorney Lotoc and NBI Director Matibag.
ORDERS OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Presiding Officer Escudero first informed the Court that the box from the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR), which had previously been ordered returned, had been delivered to the BER sealed, unopened, and duly acknowledged as received. Upon learning that the prosecution filed a motion for the issuance of a subpoena duces tecum and ad testificandum to the BIR, he instructed the parties to verify that Respondent’s counsel had been furnished a copy of the motion to facilitate its consideration.

For ensure consistent treatment of the proposed subpoena for bank records and the request for the production of the bank records of the Vice President and her spouse, Atty. Mans Carpio, Presiding Officer Escudero directed both parties to submit their respective memoranda on the proposed BIR subpoena by Monday.

He likewise scheduled oral arguments on both the BIR records and bank records issues for Wednesday, allotting each party ten (10) minutes for its principal argument and an additional three (3) minutes for rebuttal, after which the Court would resolve the matter.

He further clarified that no subpoena would be issued to NBI Director Matibag for the following week’s hearings. Upon the prosecution’s manifestation, he instead directed the issuance of a subpoena to Atty. Zuleika Lopez requiring her to appear on July 13 and 14, noting that the July 15 hearing would be devoted to the scheduled oral arguments on the subpoena for the BIR and bank documents.
WEDNESDAY, JULY 8, 2026
ADJOURNMENT OF THE IMPEACHMENT TRIAL
The Presiding Officer declared the impeachment trial adjourned until two o’clock in the afternoon of Monday, July 13, 2026.
It was 4:57 p.m.
I hereby certify to the correctness of the foregoing.
TUG JR.
^NATO N.
' Clerk of A Approved on July 13, 2026
